quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Conjugate Pairs and pH Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Conjugate Acid-Base Pairs, pH Scale, pH Calculations, pOH, Acidic vs Basic Solutions

questions.question: A conjugate acid-base pair consists of two species that differ by:
questions.explanation: According to BrГёnsted-Lowry theory, a conjugate acid-base pair differs by one proton (HвЃє). When an acid donates a proton, it becomes its conjugate base.

questions.question: In the pair Hв‚‚SOв‚„/HSOв‚„вЃ», HSOв‚„вЃ» is the:
questions.explanation: Hв‚‚SOв‚„ can donate a proton to become HSOв‚„вЃ». Therefore, HSOв‚„вЃ» is the conjugate base of the acid Hв‚‚SOв‚„.

questions.question: Which pair is NOT a conjugate acid-base pair?
questions.explanation: Hв‚‚SOв‚„ and SOв‚„ВІвЃ» differ by two protons, not one. The conjugate base of Hв‚‚SOв‚„ is HSOв‚„вЃ», not SOв‚„ВІвЃ». SOв‚„ВІвЃ» is the conjugate base of HSOв‚„вЃ».

questions.question: The pH scale typically ranges from:
questions.explanation: The pH scale typically ranges from 0 to 14, though values below 0 and above 14 are possible for extremely concentrated solutions. pH 7 is neutral, below 7 is acidic, above 7 is basic.

questions.question: Which solution has the lowest hydrogen ion concentration?
questions.explanation: pH = -log[HвЃє], so higher pH means lower [HвЃє]. pH 12 has [HвЃє] = 10вЃ»В№ВІ M, pH 8 has 10вЃ»вЃё M, pH 6 has 10вЃ»вЃ¶ M, and pH 2 has 10вЃ»ВІ M. Therefore, pH 12 has the lowest hydrogen ion concentration.

questions.question: Which one of the following will turn blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Blue litmus turns red in acidic solutions. Vinegar contains acetic acid, making it acidic. Lime water (calcium hydroxide) and baking soda solution (sodium bicarbonate) are basic. Salt solution (NaCl) is neutral and does not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which one of the following will turn red litmus blue?
questions.explanation: Red litmus turns blue in basic (alkaline) solutions. Baking soda solution (sodium bicarbonate) is basic. Vinegar and lemon juice are acidic, and salt solution is neutral вЂ” none of these turn red litmus blue.

questions.question: A solution with pH = 7 is:
questions.explanation: A solution with pH = 7 is neutral at 25В°C, meaning [HвЃє] = [OHвЃ»] = 1.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃ· M.

questions.question: Select all that apply: The pH of a solution is defined as:
questions.explanation: pH is defined as -log[HвЃє] or equivalently log(1/[HвЃє]). Both expressions give the same value.

questions.question: Which pH value indicates the most acidic solution?
questions.explanation: Lower pH values indicate more acidic solutions. pH = 2 has [HвЃє] = 0.01 M, which is more acidic than pH = 5 ([HвЃє] = 0.00001 M).

questions.question: The [HвЃє] in a solution with pH = 3 is:
The pOH of a solution is related to pH by:
questions.explanation: At 25В°C, pH + pOH = 14. This relationship comes from the ion product of water: [HвЃє][OHвЃ»] = 1.0 Г— 10вЃ»В№вЃґ.

questions.question: A solution with pOH = 5 has a pH of:
Which solution is basic?
questions.explanation: pH > 7 indicates a basic solution, where [OHвЃ»] > [HвЃє]. pH = 11 is basic.

questions.question: The [HвЃє] in a solution with pH = 9 is:
questions.explanation: [HвЃє] = 10вЃ»бµ–бґґ = 10вЃ»вЃ№ = 1 Г— 10вЃ»вЃ№ M. This very low concentration indicates a basic solution.
